VESSELS
Welcome To MMS Maritime (India)
MMS (Meikai Marine Services) was originally a part of the Meiji Shipping group in Japan.
                                It was originally Shipping Affairs Division which was later made into a Tokyo-based
                                independent unit to manage their liners technically. Hence MMS was established as a
                                separate company to streamline operations, retaining their more than 50 years of
                                experience in technical ship management, consultation for ship-building, dockyard
                                supervision, sale and purchase inspections and other related services. MMS Japan,
                                established in July 1974, is in the business services of ship management, supervision of
                                construction, remodelling, inspection and related transactions
MMS Maritime (India) Pvt. Ltd. (MMSI), was established in 2001 to support the MMS Group
                                in Japan by employing high-quality senior Indian personnel and maintaining a dedicated
                                pool of seafarers for tanker operations. It was the second maritime recruiting company
                                in India to be launched with a full Japanese capital.
The original name of MMS Maritime Agency (India) Pvt Ltd gave way to MMS Maritime
                                (India) Pvt Ltd in November 2007, to indicate its wider role as it was also transformed
                                being a company involved closely in trans operations besides only manning operations. In
                                the same year, a branch office was also set up in New Delhi.
We have several decades of experience, with more than two decades in the local shipping
                                industry, including in recruitment and training. We, therefore, have a clear
                                understanding of the industry and the technological and regulatory environment in which
                                we operate.
our services
Crewing
We provide with bespoke recruitment service of professional
                                        personnel for the maritime industry.
Training
In our training programs, we strive to equip seafarers with the top
                                        level skills for shipping.

Birth of MMSI
Home
Birth of MMSI
How MMS came to existence
MMS Co. Ltd Tokyo: Ship Management
As a ship management company of the Meiji Shipping Co., Ltd. group, its
                                            mission is to "provide the worldwide standard service with maximum safety
                                            consideration and environmental consideration." We have bases in Tokyo,
                                            Singapore, Manila, and India, providing services such as ship management,
                                            crew manning, supervision of new shipbuilding construction, etc., and
                                            received high praise from customers both in Japan and overseas. This high
                                            evaluation is based on abundant experience and knowledge accumulated through
                                            history since the establishment in 1974. In addition to having management
                                            records corresponding to a wide variety of ship types such as tankers (LNG,
                                            VLCC, Aframax, LR, MR and VLGC), Bulker (Cape to Handy), PCTC and WCC etc..
                                            in addition, crew members from the shore staff including
                                            officials/maritime/technical supervisors and from officers to members have
                                            pride that ship management is an essential task for international maritime
                                            transport, and solidarity with day and night with the aim of generating the
                                            self feeling as, “I am proud of the result that I achieve in my day to day
                                            work ”.
The company started as International Marine Consultant Co.,
                                            Ltd.,
                                            established in July 1974, and has grown based on ship management and new
                                            construction supervision for foreign ship owners. In November 1985, the
                                            company name was changed to MMS Co., Ltd., and at the same time the
                                            organization was strengthened, with the Singapore branch being opened (2009)
                                            followed by the New Delhi branch (2016) to meet diverse needs.
MMS Maritime (India) Pvt. Ltd
MMS Maritime (India) Pvt. Ltd was established in 2001 to
                                            support the MMS Group in Japan for employing high quality senior Indian
                                            personnel and maintaining a dedicated pool of seafarers for tanker
                                            operations. It was the second maritime recruiting company in India to be
                                            launched with a full Japanese capital.
The original name of MMS Maritime Agency (India) Pvt Ltd gave way to MMS
                                            Maritime (India) Pvt Ltd in November 2007, to indicate its wider role as it
                                            was transformed into a “Technical Support Center” along with just being a
                                            company involved with crewing and manning operations. In the same year, a
                                            branch office was also setup in New Delhi for better penetration into the
                                            shipping industry besides recruiting capable seafarers from the northern
                                            part of the country. There were just a few tank vessels and a bare handful
                                            of officers when MMSI started its operations. It was those officers who have
                                            exhibited their loyalty towards the company over the previous establishment
                                            and entirely supported the company in building up its system over the period
                                            of time. These officers have been the backbone of our company, and we at
                                            MMSI express our heartfelt gratitude to them for their immense contribution
                                            towards the company since then.
We have over 50 years of experience, with almost two decades of the local
                                            shipping industry, including in recruitment and training. We, therefore,
                                            have a clear understanding of the industry and the technological and
                                            regulatory environment in which we operate. The company continues to support
                                            and expand the ship management work of the whole group.
As adopters of modern technology, we have also gained invaluable insight
                                            into the needs and legitimate concerns of our website visitors and the site
                                            has evolved accordingly. We have an overriding commitment to online security
                                            and the right to confidentiality over personal or privileged/restricted
                                            information, and this has played a fundamental part in the development and
                                            management of this web site.

WHY US?
JOIN US

Mission Vission
Home
Mission Vision
Mission Statement & Code of Ethics :
We will be fair and truthful in our relations with our business
                                                    partners, colleagues, authorities and the public at large.
We will be responsible, exemplary citizens and constructive members
                                                    of
                                                    the various communities of which we are a part.
We will do the right thing with things right in all that we do and
                                                    continue to improve.
We will respect human life as always the first and choose to consider
                                                    the environment before profit.
No harassment is allowed within our organization, and
                                                    having an
                                                    arrogant attitude is also prohibited.
Vision :
To become a leading Ship manager chosen on quality by
                                                    our business partners, enabling our colleagues to work with pride,
                                                    keeping the world’s lifeline running and protecting the environment
                                                    of our beautiful planet.
Our Values
We share the following
                                                        values among us for realizing our vision/mission.
Intergrity and honesty
Open and respectful
Back to Basics
Work as Team
Go extra Mile (“Be Proactive”)
Think Why
Seek for Change
Policy
Our key policy encompasses the safety of human life, the moral issues
                                                    about protecting the environment and others, and the commitment and
                                                    dedication of our staff to any task put before us.
Environment before profit
Respect history but always look towards the future
Quick decisions, faster actions, higher efficiency
Choose performance over appearance
Harvest is always ahead
Realize Vision / Mission by sharing the
                                                        following values.
Honesty
Respect for others
Be faithful to the basics
Contribute towards teamwork
Thinking one step ahead
Question Status Quo
Seek change
Company’s Quality & Recruitment Policy
Quality Policy
Recruitment Policy
The three cornerstones
                                                    of the company policy are to achieve quality
                                                    through
Professionalism
Reliability
Integrity
The underlying basis of the above three cornerstones
                                                    are built around
                                                    the minimum regulatory requirements as outlined in the STCW 78 as
                                                    amended convention and those of the flag state.
Our stress and emphasis on self-improvement is the
                                                    direct outcome of
                                                    having a proactive approach of responsiveness and creativity towards
                                                    our customer’s needs.
To achieve the above through a uniform and consistent
                                                    application we
                                                    have established a Quality Management System that meets the
                                                    requirements of the ISO 9001:2015 standard.
Our attempts at increasing customer satisfaction span
                                                    all possible areas
                                                    of our operational activities.
Our constant endeavour is to provide cost effective and
                                                    efficient
                                                    services to our customers. In doing so we shall focus on:
Compliance with all applicable national and international
                                                        regulations
Development of Human Resources
Effective measurement / monitoring of the effectiveness of our
                                                        Quality Management System
Continual improvement in our services
Recruitment Policy
Reaffirms its opposition to unfair discrimination in
                                                    employment and
                                                    commits itself to a comprehensive policy of equal opportunity. The
                                                    aim
                                                    of this policy is to ensure that the recruitment, selection,
                                                    training
                                                    and promotion of its floating staff is based solely on the criteria
                                                    of
                                                    merit, ability and availability.
No job applicant or employee will
                                                    receive less favourable treatment on the grounds of race, colour,
                                                    ethnic
                                                    origin, age, and marital status or on the basis of any inducements,
                                                    cash
                                                    or kind, given or intended to be given, officially or otherwise. To
                                                    reaffirm, no means, mechanisms or lists intended to prevent or deter
                                                    seafarers from gaining employment would be used.

Management Team
Home
Management Team
Capt R. Yoshigae
(President)
Qualifications:
Bachelor’s degree in Maritime Science.
Working with MMSI since :
Year 2020
Capt. R Yoshigae graduated from Kobe Merchant Maritime University in 1991 and
                                                        joined Meiji Shipping. After sailing on Tankers, PCCs and Bulkers, have moved
                                                        ashore and taken over MMS Bulk Shipmanagement Singapore as Managing Director in
                                                        2017. After commanding MMS BMS for three years, then transferred to MMS Tokyo as
                                                        Managing Director in 2020. Subsequently, he was appointed as the President of
                                                        MMS. Co. Ltd & MMS Maritime India Pvt. Ltd in 2023.
Qualifications :
Class I Engineer, Masters in Maritime Law (London), Masters in Business
                                                        Management, Ph.D. in Business Management (with specialization in Human Resources
                                                        Management)
Working with MMSI since :
Inception
Professional Experience :
After having sailed out at sea, last as a Chief Engineer, on Oil Tankers, for
                                                        several years, Sanjay Bhavnani came ashore in the year 1998. After two stints on
                                                        shore, one at a marine training institute as in charge of all tanker related
                                                        training and the other at the head office of the Japanese Shipping Company in
                                                        Tokyo, he moved to the then newly formed MMS Maritime (India) Pvt Ltd in April
                                                        2002.The company has been since then involved in the crewing and the training
                                                        operations pertaining to the seafarers deployed on the tanker fleet vessels
                                                        chartered to various oil majors.
His present perspective on the crewing aspects is based on his
                                                        close interactions had with the seafarers as well as with the shore staff with
                                                        an attempt to align all expectations within an overall and an integrated
                                                        framework based on the continually increasing industry demands.He is a part of
                                                        various maritime organisations and involved in representing the views of the
                                                        industry, on matters relating to Merchant Shipping, Maritime Labour Convention,
                                                        Seafarers Identity Document, Criminalization of Seafarers, Piracy and Hijacking,
                                                        STCW matters at International Maritime Organization, Education and Training
                                                        Matters, besides other related contemporary issues.
Dr. Sanjay Bhavnani
(Director & Chief Operating Officer)
Mr. Anil Achuthan
(General Manager (Fleet Personnel))
Qualifications :
B.Tech (Mechanical), Class I Engineer
Working with MMSI since :
Year 2018
Professional Experience :
Chief Engineer with MMS, Surveyor at Indian Register of Shipping and Manager at
                                                        Maersk Line. He started his maritime career as a TME with a leading shipping
                                                        company before eventually moving to MMS where he last sailed as a Chief Engineer
                                                        on Oil Tankers. He shifted ashore in 2010 and was with a reputed classification
                                                        society and subsequently with a major container ship owning company in their
                                                        technical division. He moved to MMSI in 2018 and has been deeply involved in all
                                                        aspects of crewing and associated activities.
Qualifications :
BCOM, ICA, MBA- Finance
Working with MMSI since :
Year 2007
Professional Experience :
Mrs. Ramila Shingadia is having more than 20 years of experience in Finance &
                                                        Accounts. She has a rich experience within the shipping industry through her
                                                        extensive experience in this field along with that of corporate affairs. She has
                                                        worked with various shipping companies and has vast experience in the field of
                                                        Accounts, Finance & Corporate Affairs. She excels in improving processes and
                                                        maximizing efficiencies and plays a key role in Companies Financial management,
                                                        Crewing Budget & Corporate Affairs.
Mrs. Ramila Shingadia
(General Manager (Finance and Corporate Affairs))
Capt. S. R. Das
( Assistant General Manager-Learning & Development)
Qualifications :
Master Mariner, BSc Nautical Science, Bachelor of Laws( LLB), Presently pursuing
                                                        PhD in Climate Change.
Working with MMSI since :
November 2021
Professional Experience :
After having sailed out at Sea for more than 18 Years on Tankers including
                                                        Parcel Chemical Tankers, Aframax Crude Tankers, Bitumen Tankers & Bulk Carriers,
                                                        Capt. S.R.Das finally stepped ashore in 2015 and since then has been
                                                        passionately involved in Maritime Training, QHSE & Ship Vetting Inspections
                                                        related activities. Has extensive experience of conducting specialised Chemical
                                                        Tankers Training, SIRE 2.0, Rightship Inspection and Vessel specific Trainings
                                                        for Seafarers, joined MMSI Maritime(India) Pvt Ltd in November 2021. He
                                                        presently heads the Learning & Development ( Training) Department and the System
                                                        Department of MMSI Maritime(India) Pvt Ltd.

Our Vessels
Home
Our vessels
The company is involved in the operations
                            of various tankers and container vessels. The tank
                            vessel fleet includes gas tankers (LNG, VLGC), oil tankers (VLCC, AFRA Tankers, (VLGC ad LNG
                            Vessels), oil vessels (VLCC’s, AFRA, LR, & MR), oil and chemical (MR) and the container
                            fleet
                            includes large vessels of about 9000-10000 TEUs.
All the above vessels are chartered on a
                            long-term basis to oil majors and other leading global
                            maritime companies.
The fleet is set to expand further with
                            several oil/chemical vessels planned to be inducted as
                            new buildings delivered from various ship yards. In addition, a few chemical vessels
                            operating
                            within dedicated transportation chemical business are also lined up for induction very soon
                            within the fleet.
The above will offer significant
                            professional opportunities for seafarers to become a part of
                            our mariners pool and we would welcome all enquiries regarding this.
Our Fleet
LNG - METHANE PATRICIA CAMILA
VLGC - RED ADMIRAL
VLCC - ESTEEM ASTRO
OIL - TANKER YOKOSUKA SPIRIT
OIL/CHEMICAL TANKER - SOLAR
KATHERINE

Events
Home
Events
MARIAPP inhouse training Officers training at Hotel Svenska 2023
In order to ensure that our employees are equipped with the right kind of skills, knowledge and abilities to perform their assigned tasks, training and development plays its crucial role towards the growth and success of seafarers and company. MARIAPP inhouse training Officers training at Hotel Svenska 09th August 2023
Officers Get-Together 2023
Memorable moments shared at the Officers' Get-Together on 09JUN at the elegant Hotel Svenska! 
 #TeamBuilding #Networking #GoodTimes
Meiji Foundation Day 2023
On May 10, 2022, MMS Maritime India Pvt. Ltd. celebrated its 112th Meiji Foundation Day with tremendous passion and fervor. Foundation Day is a day of remembering, of honoring, of celebrating what has been accomplished over the years, and of looking forward to what is still to come. 
There was lot of joy and excitement amongst all. To add the joy of the occasion and make the celebration truly memorable for all, series of activities like spot prizes and Fun sport Activities like Meiji Foundation Day Fun Cricket Internal Tournament were organized which was enjoyed a lot.
Independence Day 2022
On this proud moment that we celebrate worldwide, our office has a new look all decorated and ready to celebrate the 75th Independence of our nation.
“Freedom in mind, Faith in our heart, Memories in our souls. Let's salute the Nation on Independence Day!”
Maritime Soccer League, MSL 2022
Is it ever too late to post a good memory? Throwback to when MMSI was emerged as the runner-up in the corporate football tournament “Maritime Soccer League 2022, MSL 2022"
Wait! we even bagged the best player of the tournament in the (Male and Female category)
“The team played very well and showcased excellent sportsmanship during the tournament,”
Navratri Celebration 2022
The festival of Navratri is all about colors. We at MMSI were even colorful!
The Team collectively adorned each color accordingly, few pictures of how Navratri 2022 was celebrated at the office Premises.
Diwali Celebration 2022
Diwali Dhamaal at MMS MARITIME.
We at MMSI celebrated festival of lights with exuberance and fervor.
Everyone was decked up in their best tradition attire and ramp walk was so much fun!
The competition of Rangoli, Best Dressed, Best desk Decoration and delicious snacks concluded the celebrations but not the excitement.

A glimpse to cherish!
Marex international maritime legal award 2022
We’re proud to announce that MMS Maritime India Pvt Ltd was honored with the Marex international maritime legal award for EXCELLENCE IN ADVOCACY FOR SEAFARERS’ RIGHTS. Congratulations to our MMSI team on yet another well-deserved award win!
The award is testament to our high standards of Advocacy for our very own seafarers’ rights in the maritime industry.
Sea job Indian Anchor Awards 2022
MMSI - MMS Maritime has been awarded “Best Employer for Chemical Fleet” at the Sea job Indian Anchor Awards 2022 presented by Sealine Group.
Indian Maritime Premier League ( IMPL ) Box Cricket Tournament 2022
The team brought home the runners up cup! MMSI stood first runners up at the INDIAN MARITIME PREMIUER LEAGUE( IMPL ) 2022 - Box Cricket Tournament and won the best male batter Trophy too.

Kudos to all the team members who put up a great fight! Despite the fierce tournament and head-to-head battle the support received from our fellow MMSI Staff and their families were impeccable and so encouraging!
Congratulations to MMSI
Womens day 2023
Womens day celebrated in MMSI

Why Choose MMSI?
Home
Why Choose MMSI?
The company strives to change lives for people through creating an opportunity to
                        reach and continually enhance their intrinsic potential. The company continues to grow and
                        evolve in order to deliver specialized sector experience, market knowledge, and a consultative
                        approach to induction and management of high-quality talent and continues to work forward
                        towards the future . One thing that remains unchanged is the company’s commitment to the
                        professional success of its clients, candidates, and employees. The company values of passion,
                        determination, work as a team, to enjoy what we do, and make a difference, are as evident now as
                        they’ve ever been.
Our People
People are at the heart of our business. Our purpose is
                                                    to change lives
                                                    for people through creating an opportunity to reach potential. Our
                                                    core
                                                    activity is to connect the individual with the opportunity to ensure
                                                    the
                                                    most beneficial outcome for both the parties.
Focussing on a long term association, we truly remain
                                                    action bound by it
                                                    by working backward and accordingly taking the necessary decisions
                                                    today
                                                    to ensure the same in the future.
Our group company's policy remains our guiding light at
                                                    all times:
Perform Safely
Act Righteously, and
Do with heart
Determined to learn
To help our people continuously develop and improve
                                                    their knowledge,skills, and abilities, we rolled out our innovative
                                                    online learning system in 2012. Over the years,, the system has been
                                                    refined and continuously modified to take care of all requirements
                                                    in the maximum possible effective manner. The system is designed to
                                                    include online learning modules, the ability to request and track
                                                    training, and self-help materials, including audio-visual ones. In
                                                    addition, the company lays maximum emphasis on imparting both
                                                    classroom and in-equipment training to continually enhance the
                                                    competence levels of its employees so as to enable them to meet all
                                                    external demands satisfactorily.

VESSELS
Welcome To MMS Maritime (India)
MMS (Meikai Marine Services) was originally a part of the Meiji Shipping group in Japan.
                                It was originally Shipping Affairs Division which was later made into a Tokyo-based
                                independent unit to manage their liners technically. Hence MMS was established as a
                                separate company to streamline operations, retaining their more than 50 years of
                                experience in technical ship management, consultation for ship-building, dockyard
                                supervision, sale and purchase inspections and other related services. MMS Japan,
                                established in July 1974, is in the business services of ship management, supervision of
                                construction, remodelling, inspection and related transactions
MMS Maritime (India) Pvt. Ltd. (MMSI), was established in 2001 to support the MMS Group
                                in Japan by employing high-quality senior Indian personnel and maintaining a dedicated
                                pool of seafarers for tanker operations. It was the second maritime recruiting company
                                in India to be launched with a full Japanese capital.
The original name of MMS Maritime Agency (India) Pvt Ltd gave way to MMS Maritime
                                (India) Pvt Ltd in November 2007, to indicate its wider role as it was also transformed
                                being a company involved closely in trans operations besides only manning operations. In
                                the same year, a branch office was also set up in New Delhi.
We have several decades of experience, with more than two decades in the local shipping
                                industry, including in recruitment and training. We, therefore, have a clear
                                understanding of the industry and the technological and regulatory environment in which
                                we operate.
our services
Crewing
We provide with bespoke recruitment service of professional

Training
In our training programs, we strive to equip seafarers with the top

Vision & Mission
Home
Vision & Mission
Mission :
To keep at its centre
                                                    the focus on people by facilitating diverse thinking coupled with a
                                                    continual competence enhancement and thereby achieve a long term
                                                    association.
                                                    To attract & retain a highly dedicated pool of qualified officers
                                                    and provide world- class service to our customers.
Vision :
To become a leading
                                                    company in the maritime industry and become the most preferred
                                                    employer and a leader in adopting sound HR practices.
Our Values
We share the following
                                                        values among us for realizing our vision/mission.
Intergrity and honesty
Open and respectful
Back to Basics
Work as Team
Go extra Mile (“Be Proactive”)
Think Why
Seek for Change
Policy
Our key policy encompasses the safety of human life, the moral issues
                                                    about protecting the environment and others, and the commitment and
                                                    dedication of our staff to any task put before us.
Environment before profit
Respect history but always look towards the future
Quick decisions, faster actions, higher efficiency
Choose performance over appearance
Harvest is always ahead
Realize Vision / Mission by sharing the
                                                        following values.
Honesty
Respect for others
Be faithful to the basics
Contribute towards teamwork
Thinking one step ahead
Question Status Quo
Seek change
Company’s Quality & Recruitment Policy
Quality Policy
Recruitment Policy
The three cornerstones
                                                    of the company policy are to achieve quality
                                                    through
Professionalism
Reliability
Integrity
The underlying basis of the above three cornerstones
                                                    are built around
                                                    the minimum regulatory requirements as outlined in the STCW 78 as
                                                    amended convention and those of the flag state.
Our stress and emphasis on self-improvement is the
                                                    direct outcome of
                                                    having a proactive approach of responsiveness and creativity towards
                                                    our customer’s needs.
To achieve the above through a uniform and consistent
                                                    application we
                                                    have established a Quality Management System that meets the
                                                    requirements of the ISO 9001:2015 standard.
Our attempts at increasing customer satisfaction span
                                                    all possible areas
                                                    of our operational activities.
Our constant endeavour is to provide cost effective and
                                                    efficient
                                                    services to our customers. In doing so we shall focus on:
Compliance with all applicable national and international
                                                        regulations
Development of Human Resources
Effective measurement / monitoring of the effectiveness of our
                                                        Quality Management System
Continual improvement in our services
Recruitment Policy
Reaffirms its opposition to unfair discrimination in
                                                    employment and
                                                    commits itself to a comprehensive policy of equal opportunity. The
                                                    aim
                                                    of this policy is to ensure that the recruitment, selection,
                                                    training
                                                    and promotion of its floating staff is based solely on the criteria
                                                    of
                                                    merit, ability and availability.
No job applicant or employee will
                                                    receive less favourable treatment on the grounds of race, colour,
                                                    ethnic
                                                    origin, age, and marital status or on the basis of any inducements,
                                                    cash
                                                    or kind, given or intended to be given, officially or otherwise. To
                                                    reaffirm, no means, mechanisms or lists intended to prevent or deter
                                                    seafarers from gaining employment would be used.
